evals.prompt: make me a github readme banner for my project 'TaskForge' — 1280x256, dark theme with a purple gradient, title and a one-line subtitle. ill embed it as in the readme so it needs to render right and not have weird whitespace around it
evals.expected_output: Writes an SVG with viewBox='0 0 1280 256', width=1280, height=256, a linearGradient using hardcoded hex colors (no CSS vars), background fills edge-to-edge, title + subtitle text. Then verifies the result by either starting the preview server or using chrome-devtools MCP to screenshot — does not just write the file and stop.

assertions.text: viewBox starts at 0 0 with the requested 1280×256 dimensions
outer SVG width and height match the banner dimensions
uses a linearGradient with hardcoded hex stops (not CSS variables)
verifies the output visually rather than writing-and-stopping (preview server, chrome-devtools MCP screenshot, or opens localhost)

evals.prompt:
this banner has whitespace around it that i cant get rid of when i embed it on github. ive tried object-fit:contain and negative margins but the padding is baked in. fix the file.

current svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="120 40 660 420" width="660" height="420">
  <rect x="120" y="40" width="660" height="420" fill="#7268F0"/>
  <text x="450" y="240" text-anchor="middle" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="40" fill="#FFFFFF">Hello</text>
</svg>
evals.expected_output: Identifies that the viewBox is offset (starts at 120 40 instead of 0 0), shifts every child element back to origin (rect to x=0 y=0, text x to 330 y to 200 — subtracting the 120/40 offset), and rewrites the viewBox to '0 0 660 420'. Explains briefly why offsets bake padding into the asset.

assertions.text: rewrites viewBox to start at 0 0 with the same 660×420 extent
shifts the background rect back to the origin (x=0 y=0)
shifts the text element by subtracting the 120/40 offset (x≈330, y≈200) — not still at 450/240
explains the rule (mentions origin / offset / 0 0 — not just rewrites silently)

evals.prompt: i have icon.svg in this folder and i need a png version for a slide deck. before i convert it i want to actually look at the svg in a browser to make sure it renders right — what's the workflow?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user (or runs) the preview server to serve icon.svg locally, optionally drives chrome-devtools MCP to navigate + screenshot for the agent's own verification, then runs the sharp-based converter (or `node scripts/convert.mjs`) to produce icon.png. Does not jump straight to conversion without previewing.

assertions.text: uses or references the skill's preview server (preview-server.mjs / serve / localhost)
uses or references the skill's converter (convert.mjs or sharp's SVG → PNG path)
produces the requested icon.png as the output
actually previews before converting — doesn't just convert blind
